message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker timestamps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -2784,7 +2784,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.12","title":"[connectors] Canonical connector event/data model + schema evolution tooling","description":"Define canonical normalized schemas for connector events/actions/outcomes, with schema evolution tooling, compatibility checks, and search/policy-friendly indexing contracts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Canonical schemas cover connector ingress, processing, and egress outcomes. 2. Versioning and compatibility checks prevent schema drift breakage. 3. Index/storage contracts support policy reasoning and forensic search. 4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e schema migration tests and detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticWolf","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:23:59.567891Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:25:21.755365Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T04:25:21.755312Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","data-model","schemas","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:23:59.567891Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:09.072770Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:11.213299Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:13.497065Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1857,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RusticWolf claiming. All deps resolved: ft-3681t.5.1 CLOSED, ft-3681t.5.7 complete (OliveHarbor), ft-3681t.5.6 complete (RusticWolf). Building canonical event/data model with schema evolution.","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:16:17Z"},{"id":1858,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RusticWolf: Implementation complete. connector_event_model.rs committed (5278b80a). 26 tests passing, 0 warnings. Types: CanonicalConnectorEvent, SchemaVersion, EventDirection, CanonicalSeverity, SchemaEvolutionRegistry, SchemaFieldDef, SchemaValidationResult, CompatibilityReport, IndexingContract. Conversion helpers for inbound signals, outbound actions, and lifecycle transitions. Schema v1 with 13 field definitions, validation, and compatibility checking.","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:23:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.13","title":"[quality] Connector integration testbed + chaos and audit verifiers","description":"Stand up a connector testbed (mock providers + real-provider canaries) with chaos scenarios, sandbox escape attempts, delivery failure storms, and audit-chain integrity checks.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Testbed validates connector lifecycle/security/bridge behaviors end-to-end. 2. Chaos scenarios verify containment and recovery without control-plane compromise. 3. Audit verifiers ensure every connector action is attributable and searchable. 4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e/chaos scripts with detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via  {"id":"ft-3681t.5.14","title":"[connectors] Data classification, redaction, and privacy-preserving event ingestion","description":"Introduce payload classification/redaction controls so connector data entering logs/search/policy stores is privacy-safe and compliance-ready without losing operational utility.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sensitive data classes are identified and handled by explicit policies. 2. Redaction/tokenization is enforced before persistence and external fanout. 3. Operator/audit workflows retain necessary forensic value without exposing secrets/PII. Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic. Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario. Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case. Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code. Q5.  {"id":"ft-3681t.5.2","title":"[connectors] Registry client and signed package verification pipeline","description":"Implement connector registry interaction with signature/manifest verification, transparency-log provenance checks, and explicit trust policy gates before install/upgrade/activation so supply-chain risk is controlled by default.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Connector artifacts are verified (signature, manifest, provenance) before activation.
2. Trust failures (revoked keys, stale attestations, missing provenance) are surfaced with actionable diagnostics.
3. Update flow supports canary + rollback without leaving partially activated states.
4. Trust-root and key-rotation policy is explicit and auditable for operators.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. No functional code changes made in this pass; task remains unverified in this environment.","created_at":"2026-03-04T03:11:59Z"},{"id":1861,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Closed by OliveHarbor. connector_registry.rs committed 0c52e68a. 52 tests passing, zero clippy warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:35:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.3","title":"[connectors] Sandbox zone execution and capability envelopes","description":"Adopt connector sandbox/zone isolation model to constrain runtime behavior (filesystem/network/exec/capabilities) and prevent connector blast-radius from compromising terminal control plane.","acceptance_criteria":"1. This resolves E0063 missing-field compile failure encountered during validation runs. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-fuchsia-handoff rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_connector_host_runtime --no-run PASS.","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:18:08Z"},{"id":1828,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.3","author":"WindyWaterfall","text":"Implemented sandbox-zone + capability-envelope execution controls in connector_host_runtime: new capability/zone/decision/request models, config validation, fail-closed authorize_operation path, enriched operation envelope metadata, bounded decision history, and health snapshot fields. Added unit + integration coverage and e2e harness tests/e2e/test_ft_3681t_5_3_connector_sandbox_zones.sh (log artifact tests/e2e/logs/ft_3681t_5_3_20260303_175548.jsonl). Validation via rch: PASS cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib connector_host_runtime_sandbox_, PASS cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_host_runtime_integration connector_host_runtime_integration_sandbox_, PASS e2e script. Workspace gates currently fail on pre-existing unrelated issues (frankenterm-gui benchmarking unresolved refs, frankenterm-core tui/views E0499 borrow errors, broad pre-existing clippy/fmt failures).","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:34:42Z"},{"id":1829,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.3","author":"WindyWaterfall","text":"Recheck update: reran rch exec cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets; current blockers are unrelated compile errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/resize_scheduler.rs (line 3174 invalid '_' expression placeholder; line 1058 wrong field 'by_seq' for ActiveCancelledSuperseded; expected superseded_by_seq). Prior tui/views E0499 blocker appears resolved by concurrent work. Keeping this bead blocked pending unrelated compile-fix landing.","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:43:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.3.1","title":"[build] Fix resize_scheduler compile blockers (invalid '_' expr + superseded_by_seq field)","description":"Resolve current compile blockers in crates/frankenterm-core/src/resize_scheduler.rs referenced by ft-3681t.5.3 notes (invalid '_' expression placeholder near line ~3174 and incorrect field usage around superseded_by_seq).
